Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Look up Hana Kim's contact details using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Hana Kim") to confirm sender address hana.kim@moveops.com for outgoing emails.
OE 2: Search QuickBooks invoices for DocNumber INV-2026-0401 using quickbooks_search_invoices (criteria: {"DocNumber": "INV-2026-0401"}) to confirm the collision - finds two distinct invoices: id 1007 (Mosaic Robotics, $4,500, TxnDate 2026-04-02) and id 1009 (Canopy Health, $9,210, TxnDate 2026-04-04).
OE 3: Read the Mosaic invoice using quickbooks_read_invoice (invoice_id: "1007") to capture the line item for the reissue - finds CustomerRef cust_mosaic, TotalAmt $4,500, single line "Standard Relocation Package - Andre Washington, Columbus GA -> Seattle WA", Balance $4,500 (unpaid).
OE 4: Read the Canopy invoice using quickbooks_read_invoice (invoice_id: "1009") to confirm it is the one that already went out to Patricia with the surcharge - finds CustomerRef cust_canopy, TotalAmt $9,210, BillEmail patricia.langford@canopyhealth.com, Balance $9,210 (unpaid), and the three non-standard surcharge line items totaling $2,650.
OE 5: Look up the Mosaic Robotics account owner using airtable_search_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "Client Accounts", field_name: "Client", value: "Mosaic Robotics") to find Account Manager Emeka Diallo so the heads-up goes to the lower-risk reissue target.
OE 6: Look up Emeka Diallo's email using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Emeka Diallo") to find emeka.diallo@moveops.com.
OE 7: Create the replacement Mosaic invoice under a clean unused DocNumber using quickbooks_create_invoice (customer_ref: "cust_mosaic", line_items: same single line as id 1007 totaling $4,500, txn_date: "2026-04-11", doc_number: new clean number such as "INV-2026-0413") so Mosaic's copy carries a unique number.
OE 8: Book a reversing entry against the original Mosaic INV-2026-0401 using quickbooks_create_invoice (customer_ref: "cust_mosaic", line_items: -$4,500 reversal lines describing VOID of duplicate DocNumber, txn_date: "2026-04-11", doc_number: "CM-2026-0411-MOSAIC" or similar) to offset the original on the books - the QB MCP has no create_credit_memo tool, so the reversing entry follows the universe pattern of CM-2026-0415 (negative-amount invoice).
OE 9: Send the heads-up email to Emeka using send_email (sender: "hana.kim@moveops.com", recipients: ["emeka.diallo@moveops.com"]) explaining the duplicate DocNumber collision, the new Mosaic DocNumber, the credit memo voiding the original, and asking him to watch for any Mosaic payment referencing the old INV-2026-0401.
OE 10: Search the finance Slack channel for prior context on the $2,650 Canopy surcharge using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Canopy surcharge INV-2026-0401") to find Hana's April 4 #finance post announcing INV-2026-0401 ($9,210, $2,650 in non-standard surcharges pending Canopy approval) and Marcus's follow-up flagging that if Patricia's approval slips past April 7 it puts vendor commitments at risk before the April 10 Marcus Webb cold-chain pickup.
OE 11: Search CRM for Canopy Health using crm_search_companies (name: "Canopy Health") to find company_canopy.
OE 12: List CRM engagements on the Canopy company record using crm_list_engagements (company_ids: ["company_canopy"]) to find eng_canopy_surcharge_approval_flag_001 titled "ACTION REQUIRED - Canopy Health: $2,650 in Invoice Surcharges Pending Patricia Langford's Approval", confirming the flag is still open.
OE 13: Search inbound email for any reply from Patricia on the surcharge using search_emails (query: "Patricia Langford INV-2026-0401 surcharge", folder_name: "INBOX") to confirm no reply has been received from patricia.langford@canopyhealth.com since the April 4 send.
OE 14: Confirm Patricia Langford's canonical email using crm_search_contacts (full_name: "Patricia Langford") to find patricia.langford@canopyhealth.com (Director of Human Resources at Canopy Health) - the address matching the QB BillEmail of record on invoice 1009.
OE 15: Send the chaser email to Patricia using send_email (sender: "hana.kim@moveops.com", recipients: ["patricia.langford@canopyhealth.com"]) referencing the April 4 invoice send, the $2,650 non-standard surcharge line items, and requesting written approval or rejection before April close.
OE 16: Book a credit memo against the Canopy $2,650 surcharge on invoice 1009 using quickbooks_create_invoice (customer_ref: "cust_canopy", line_items: -$2,650 reversal lines describing credit memo against the three surcharge line items pending Patricia's written approval, txn_date: "2026-04-11", doc_number: "CM-2026-0411-CANOPY-SURCHARGE" or similar) so the receivable carried into close matches what Canopy is willing to pay.
OE 17: Read the Terraform preliminary estimate using quickbooks_read_invoice (invoice_id: "est_terraform_q2_batch1_2026") to confirm it is still in estimate form - finds DocNumber EST-2026-0423, CustomerRef cust-terraform-001, TotalAmt $6,560, TxnDate 2026-04-23, DueDate 2026-05-23.
OE 18: Search QuickBooks for Terraform / Vantage Distributed invoices using quickbooks_search_invoices (criteria: {"customer_ref": "cust-terraform-001"}) to confirm only NMD-2026-0042 ($462,500 parent Enterprise Package from 2026-03-18) and EST-2026-0423 itself exist - no batch invoice has been booked against the estimate.
OE 19: Get the Terraform customer record using quickbooks_get_customer (id: "cust-terraform-001") to verify Vantage Distributed, balance $0 - customer state still reflects estimate-only.
OE 20: Create a Linear close-checklist reminder ticket using linear_create_issue (team: "team_operations", title: referencing EST-2026-0423 Terraform hold - do not convert until volume discount finalized, description: noting Marcus's directive after the April 22 reply-all pricing leak and instructing the May close to re-verify no conversion, assignee: "moveops_hana_kim", priority: 2) so the hold carries into next month.
OE 21: Read the KeyMove rider bill using quickbooks_get_bill (id: "BILL-KEYMOVE-2026-0417") to capture DocNumber KM-44192-ICR, VendorRef VEND-KEYMOVE-001, TotalAmt $1,200, TxnDate 2026-04-17, posted against account ACC-6185 "Claims & Remediation Expense".
OE 22: List Airtable bases and tables using airtable_list_bases and airtable_list_tables (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001") to find base appMoveOpsOps001 with tables tblRelocations01, tblStipends00001, and tblClientAccts01.
OE 23: Search Airtable Relocations for Emilia Cruz using airtable_search_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "Relocations", field_name: "Name", value: "Emilia Cruz") to find recEmiliaCruzChicagoDenver - NorthWind Technologies, Chicago -> Denver, Move Start 2026-04-14, Move End 2026-04-18, Coordinator Blessing Okafor, Status "In Progress" (not closed cleanly), Special Requirements covering the Steinway Model B grand piano.
OE 24: Search the NorthWind CRM record using crm_search_companies (name: "NorthWind Technologies") then crm_list_engagements (company_ids: ["company_northwind"]) to find the Emilia Cruz inbound piano damage complaint call (crm_engagement_0d8a9121b53c) and the NorthWind Denver expansion engagement history.
OE 25: Update the Cruz Airtable record using airtable_update_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_id: "tblRelocations01", records: [{"id": "recEmiliaCruzChicagoDenver", "fields": {"Special Requirements": existing Steinway text appended with a memo line referencing KeyMove rider bill KM-44192-ICR, $1,200, account ACC-6185 Claims & Remediation, piano damage incident}}]) so the auditor can trace the rider expense back to the in-progress move.
OE 26: Create a CRM engagement note on NorthWind using crm_create_engagement (engagement_type: "NOTE", company_ids: ["company_northwind"], title: referencing KeyMove rider tie-out, body: tying KeyMove bill KM-44192-ICR to the Emilia Cruz Steinway damage during the Chicago -> Denver relocation and noting ops still needs to close the Airtable status) to give the account team the paper trail.
OE 27: Post the single close-week recap in #finance using conversations_add_message (channel_id: "C005", payload: covering (a) Mosaic INV-2026-0401 reissued under the new clean DocNumber with credit memo voiding the original and heads-up sent to Emeka, (b) Canopy $2,650 surcharge - chaser sent to Patricia and credit memo booked against invoice 1009 pending her written approval, (c) Terraform EST-2026-0423 verified estimate-only with May close-checklist reminder filed, (d) KeyMove rider KM-44192-ICR tied out to the Emilia Cruz NorthWind relocation with Airtable memo line and CRM engagement note, plus a flag that ops still needs to close the Cruz relocation cleanly) so Marcus, Alejandro, and the AMs start close week aligned.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

📋 Rubrics
Rubric Results
✓ Pass ✗ Fail Click any row to expand justifications
# Criterion Run #1Run #2Run #3Run #4Run #5Run #6 Pass Rate
1 The agent selects a QuickBooks read tool (e.g., quickbooks_search_invoices, quickbooks_read_invoice, quickbooks_search_bills, quickbooks_get_bill, quickbooks_search_accounts, or quickbooks_get_customer) to pull invoice, bill, customer, and account records from the books.
tool selection
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The agent called multiple QuickBooks read tools: quickbooks_search_invoices, quickbooks_search_customers, quickbooks_search_bills, quickbooks_search_accounts, quickbooks_get_customer, and quickbooks_read_invoice. All four workstreams were investigated using QuickBooks data.
✓Run #2The agent called multiple QuickBooks read tools: quickbooks_search_invoices, quickbooks_search_customers (multiple times), quickbooks_search_bills, quickbooks_search_accounts, and quickbooks_read_invoice. This criterion is clearly met.
✓Run #3The agent called multiple QuickBooks read tools: quickbooks_search_invoices, quickbooks_read_invoice (twice, for IDs 1007 and 1009), quickbooks_search_bills, and quickbooks_search_accounts. These satisfy the criterion.
✓Run #4The agent called multiple QuickBooks read tools: quickbooks_search_invoices, quickbooks_read_invoice (for invoices 1007 and 1009), quickbooks_search_bills, quickbooks_search_accounts, quickbooks_get_bill (KM-44192-ICR), and quickbooks_get_customer (cust-terraform-001). All clearly evident in the trajectory.
✓Run #5The agent called multiple QuickBooks read tools: quickbooks_search_invoices, quickbooks_search_bills, quickbooks_search_accounts, and quickbooks_read_invoice (for invoice id 1007).
✓Run #6The agent called multiple QuickBooks read tools including quickbooks_search_invoices (with empty criteria to get all invoices), quickbooks_search_customers (multiple calls for Mosaic, Canopy, Terraform, KeyMove), quickbooks_search_bills (criteria TxnDate >= 2026-04-01), quickbooks_search_accounts (for claims-related accounts), and quickbooks_get_customer.
2 The agent selects an Airtable read tool (e.g., airtable_list_bases, airtable_list_tables, airtable_list_records, or airtable_search_records) to look up the Mosaic Robotics client account owner and the Emilia Cruz relocation record tied to the KeyMove rider.
tool selection
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
